title: CIL Industrial Engineering — Full-length Mock 07

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Education is the key to personal and social development. Q: It empowers individuals to make informed decisions. R: Quality education should be accessible to all. S: Governments must invest in educational infrastructure.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The city is known ____ its coal deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Natural disasters cause widespread destruction. Q: Early warning systems can reduce the impact. R: Preparedness and planning are essential. S: Governments invest in disaster management infrastructure.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was completed ____ schedule.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Sustainable development balances economic growth and environmental protection. Q: The concept gained global attention after the Brundtland Report. R: It emphasises meeting present needs without compromising future generations. S: The UN Sustainable Development Goals outline specific targets.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is popular ____ his colleagues.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Stock markets reflect the health of an economy. Q: Share prices fluctuate based on supply and demand. R: Investors buy and sell shares of publicly listed companies. S: Market regulators ensure fair trading practices.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The patient died ____ a heart attack.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Democracy is a system of government by the people. Q: Citizens elect their representatives through free and fair elections. R: These representatives make laws on behalf of the people. S: An independent judiciary ensures that laws are followed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is capable ____ doing the job independently.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Amazon rainforest is often called the lungs of the Earth. Q: It produces about 20% of the world's oxygen. R: Deforestation rates have increased in recent decades. S: International efforts are underway to protect the rainforest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is interested ____ pursuing higher studies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Open-pit mining is used when coal seams are near the surface. Q: The topsoil and overburden are removed first. R: The coal is then extracted using heavy machinery. S: After mining, the land is reclaimed for other uses.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She speaks ____ softly that no one can hear her.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Coal is formed over millions of years. Q: Under heat and pressure, peat transforms into coal. R: Dead plant material accumulates in swamps forming peat. S: This process is called coalification.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The road is lined ____ trees on both sides.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Vaccines have saved millions of lives worldwide. Q: They work by training the immune system to fight diseases. R: Herd immunity protects those who cannot be vaccinated. S: Vaccine development requires rigorous clinical trials.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: government / measures / safety / implemented / has / stringent
options: Stringent safety measures government has implemented
Government has implemented stringent safety measures
Safety measures stringent implemented has government
Implemented has government stringent safety measures
questions.solution: Government has implemented stringent safety measures

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of LETHARGIC:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The printing press revolutionised the spread of information. Q: It was invented by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century. R: Books became more affordable and widely available. S: This led to increased literacy rates across Europe.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRAGMATIC:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager ____ the proposal before the board.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of JUBILANT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He ____ the opportunity to study abroad.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of DILIGENT:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Life cycle cost (LCC):
options: Acquisition + operation + maintenance + disposal. Energy, downtime, spares cost
questions.solution: LCC: 30% acquisition, 60% ops/maintenance, 10% disposal. NPV and IRR comparison.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Master Production Schedule (MPS) is disaggregated from:
questions.solution: MPS: specific products and quantities per time period. Derived from APP. Input to MRP. Balances demand and capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good material-handling system aims to:
options: Move material efficiently while minimizing handling
questions.solution: The principle is least handling: move the right material efficiently and safely.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LIFE cycle costing:
options: Acquisition + ownership + disposal cost over product life
questions.solution: LCC: initial cost (30%) + operating/maintenance (60%) + disposal (10%). VE targets high operating cost.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Factory flow analysis: from-to chart shows:
questions.solution: From-to: matrix of flow volumes between department pairs. High-flow pairs should be adjacent. Drives layout.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loading:
options: Assigning work to work centers (machines/people). Machine loading chart
questions.solution: Loading: capacity check. Infinite loading (ignore capacity) vs finite loading (consider capacity).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Weibull shape parameter β < 1 indicates:
questions.solution: Weibull: β<1 decreasing (burn-in), β=1 constant (random), β>1 increasing (wear-out). Flexible model.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
options: Environmental management. EMS framework. Reduce environmental impact
questions.solution: ISO 14001: environmental policy, planning, implementation, checking, management review.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics — RULA (Rapid Upper Limb Assessment):
options: Score A (upper arm, lower arm, wrist) + score B (neck, trunk, legs) + muscle/force. Grand score 1-7
questions.solution: RULA: McAtamney & Corlett (1993). Score > 4 = investigate. Score > 7 = change immediately.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vehicle routing problem (VRP):
options: Capacity (CVRP), Time window (VRPTW), Multi-depot (MDVRP). Clarke-Wright savings heuristic
questions.solution: VRP: VRP = multiple TSPs. CVRP: customer demand + vehicle capacity. Route construction + improvement.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Littles Law relates:
options: WIP, throughput rate, and flow time

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Confidence interval for simulation output width depends on:
questions.solution: Width ∝ σ/√n. More replications = narrower CI. Higher variance = wider CI. Can also use batch means for steady-state.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 5 min with 15% allowances. Its standard time is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Genetic algorithm crossover operator's purpose:
questions.solution: Crossover: exchange genes between parents. Exploits good building blocks. Probability pc. Types: single-point, uniform, PMX.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multi-echelon inventory — base-stock policy:
options: Each stage orders to bring inventory position to base stock level. Echelon = stage + all downstream
questions.solution: Multi-echelon: Clark-Scarf (1960). Echelon base-stock. Inventory + pipeline at all downstream stages.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DFMA (Design for Manufacture and Assembly):
options: Integrated DFM + DFA. Minimum manufacturing and assembly cost
questions.solution: DFMA: concurrent engineering. Boothroyd-Dewhurst DFMA software. Cost reduction 30-50%.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) system advantages:
options: Flexible routing, reduced labor, consistent throughput
questions.solution: AGVs: automated material transport. Flexible paths. Lower labor. Require maintenance. ROI analysis needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Warehouse layout — golden zone:
options: Middle shelves at waist-to-shoulder height, middle of aisles. High-pick items here
questions.solution: Golden zone: ergonomic efficiency. Slotting: ABC items in best locations. Forward pick vs reserve.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Measurement System Analysis: Gage R&R studies:
options: Variability from measurement system vs total variability
questions.solution: Gage R&R: %GRR < 10% acceptable, 10-30% marginal, >30% unacceptable. Studies repeatability and reproducibility.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supplier development:
options: Investing in supplier capabilities. Long-term partnership. Training, technology transfer
questions.solution: Supplier development: Toyota approach. Supplier parks. Collaborative improvement.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Conway's law: organization design determines:
questions.solution: Conway: systems mirror the communication structure of the org. Microservices → small autonomous teams. Important for design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Psychophysical approach to work design uses:
questions.solution: Psychophysical: uses worker-reported acceptable workload. Based on perception of fatigue, discomfort. Good for design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.2, last actual = 250 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory — dead stock:
options: Obsolete, no demand. Should be written off or disposed
questions.solution: Dead stock: no future use. Holding cost wasted. Causes: overbuying, product changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The reliability of a system of components in series is:
options: The product of the component reliabilities
questions.solution: Series system reliability Rs = R₁·R₂·…·Rn; it is lower than any single component.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Throughput Accounting: inventory is:
options: Money invested in things not yet sold
questions.solution: Inventory = all money in system (RM, WIP, FG, equipment). Goal: minimize inventory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cellular manufacturing:
options: Group Technology (GT). Machines grouped by part family. Reduced setup, WIP
questions.solution: GT: production flow analysis (PFA). Cell: from-to chart. U-shaped cells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Master Production Schedule (MPS):
options: Planned production quantities per period. Drives MRP
questions.solution: MPS: end items. Time-phased. Aggregate plan disaggregated. Rough-cut capacity planning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corrective maintenance: maintenance performed after failure occurs.
questions.solution: Corrective: fix when broken. Lower planned cost, higher unplanned cost (downtime, secondary damage).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 8, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — X-bar:
options: Monitors process mean (average of samples). Centerline = grand mean
questions.solution: X-bar: UCL = X̄ + A₂·R̄. LCL = X̄ - A₂·R̄. Sample size typically 4-6.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 10/hour and spend on average 1 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Just-in-Time (JIT):
options: Zero inventory philosophy. Pull system (Kanban). Takt time. Continuous flow
questions.solution: JIT: eliminate waste. 7 wastes (Muda). Kanban: signal card. Level production (Heijunka).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 6400/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹4/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 506) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 2000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory — EOQ:
options: Economic Order Quantity = √(2DS/H). Minimizes total cost (ordering + holding)
questions.solution: EOQ assumptions: constant demand, no lead time, instantaneous replenishment. D = demand, S = setup, H = holding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.2, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FMEA — RPN interpretation:
options: S (severity 1-10), O (occurrence 1-10), D (detection 1-10). RPN = S×O×D. RPN > 100 = action required
questions.solution: FMEA: risk priority number. Severity: 9-10 (safety). Occurrence: 10 (very high). Detection: 10 (undetectable).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line balancing: primary objective:
options: Minimize cycle time or maximize output rate for given tasks and precedence
questions.solution: Line balance: assign tasks to stations. Minimize idle time. Balance delay = idle/(n×CT).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Neural network forecasting:
options: Non-linear. Input lags → hidden layer → output. Universal approximator
questions.solution: NN: MLP with backpropagation. Time series: lagged values as inputs. Ensemble methods.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Just-in-Time (JIT) vs Just-in-Case (JIC):
options: JIT: minimize inventory, respond to demand. JIC: buffer inventory for uncertainty
questions.solution: JIT: lean, low waste. JIC: risk-averse, high inventory. Trade-off: cost vs service.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 50 units/day with a lead time of 5 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 50×5 = 250 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 3, tm = 5, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Economic Order Quantity (EOQ): Q* = √(2DS/H). For D=10000, S=$50, H=$2/unit/year:
questions.solution: EOQ: balances ordering and holding cost. Q* = √(2×10000×50/2) ≈ 707.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity analysis: objective coefficient ranging:
options: Range over which current basis remains optimal (allowable increase/decrease in c_j)
questions.solution: Obj coefficient range: current basis optimal within range. Slope of objective function doesn't cross vertex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Motion economy — at workplace:
options: Tools/materials pre-positioned. Gravity feed. Drop delivery. Proper height
questions.solution: Workplace: forearm motion preferred. Semi-circular layout. Adjustable chair.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹80, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 30000/(80 − 60) = 1500 units, where total revenue = total cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the control chart shown (CL = 70, UCL = 79, LCL = 61), the highlighted point lying above the UCL indicates that the process is:
options: Out of control (an assignable cause is present)
In control with only chance variation
questions.solution: A point beyond the ±3σ control limits (UCL = 79) signals an assignable cause — the process is out of statistical control.

questions.stem: Group Technology — coding:
options: Optiz (5 digits: shape, form elements, dimension, material, accuracy). For part family identification
questions.solution: Optiz: hierarchical. 1st digit: part class (rotational, non-rotational). 2nd: form elements. 3rd: dimension. 4th: material. 5th: accuracy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depreciation in engineering economics represents:
options: The decline in value of an asset over time
questions.solution: Depreciation allocates an asset's cost over its useful life as it loses value.

options: Adjustable height (sit-stand). Monitor at eye level. Wrist straight. Footrest if needed
questions.solution: Ergonomics: reduce MSD (musculoskeletal disorders). RSI (repetitive strain injury).

In control with only chance variation

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Input-Output control:
options: Monitor actual vs planned throughput at work centers. Backlog (queue) control. Cumulative I-O deviation
questions.solution: I-O: backlog = cumulative input — cumulative output. Deviation → capacity adjustment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work factor (WF):
options: Predetermined time. Body member, distance, manual control, weight/resistance
questions.solution: WF: simpler than MTM. Motion time data. Ready Work Factor: simplified version.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RCM — failure consequences:
options: Hidden: no direct effect (backup fails). Safety: injury/death. Environmental: violation. Operational: downtime
questions.solution: RCM: proactive (PM, PdM) vs default (run-to-failure, redesign). Decision tree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SDE (Scarce-Difficult-Easy):
options: By availability/lead time. S: scarce (imported). D: difficult. E: easy (local)
questions.solution: SDE: for procurement. S: long lead time, advance purchase. E: local, short lead time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Every linear programming problem (the primal) has an associated:
options: Dual problem with related optimal value
questions.solution: By LP duality, the primal and dual optima are equal (strong duality) when both are feasible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 88, mean = 102, σ = 3. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NIOSH lifting equation:
options: RWL = LC × HM × VM × DM × FM × AM × CM. LI = Load/RWL. LI > 1 → risky
questions.solution: NIOSH: 1991 revised. LC = 23 kg. Multipliers (0-1). Lifting Index (LI). Asymmetric multiplier.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Taguchi methods:
options: Robust design. Signal-to-noise (S/N) ratio. Parameter design to reduce variation
questions.solution: Taguchi: loss function, orthogonal arrays. Nominal-best, smaller-better, larger-better S/N.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stepping stone method: closed loop for unoccupied cell consists of:
options: Alternating horizontal and vertical moves connecting occupied cells
questions.solution: Stepping stone: trace loop for entering variable. Each corner must have occupied cell (except entering).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Markov chains:
options: State transitions with memoryless property. Steady-state probabilities
questions.solution: Markov: transition matrix P. Steady state: π·P = π. π_i = proportion of time in state i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Markov — absorbing state:
options: P_ii = 1 (once entered, never left). Fundamental matrix: (I-Q)^(-1)
questions.solution: Absorbing: eventual absorption probability. Expected time to absorption. Mean first passage time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Monte Carlo simulation:
options: Random sampling. Input distributions → output distribution. Risk analysis, stochastic modeling
questions.solution: MC: @RISK, Crystal Ball. Latin Hypercube sampling. 10,000+ iterations. Output: histogram, percentiles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 50 units/day with a lead time of 10 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 50×10 = 500 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 3600/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹2/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 536.7) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 30 units/day with a lead time of 7 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 30×7 = 210 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Economic Order Quantity (EOQ) minimizes the sum of:
questions.solution: EOQ balances annual ordering cost against annual holding cost to minimize total inventory cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Value engineering: Value = Function / Cost. To increase value:
options: Increase function or decrease cost (or both)
questions.solution: VE: improve value per unit cost. Function analysis identifies what customer pays for.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 90, mean = 100, σ = 3. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical path:
options: Longest path through network. Zero float activities. Determines project duration
questions.solution: Critical path: any delay → project delay. May have multiple critical paths.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecasting types:
options: Qualitative (judgment, Delphi) and Quantitative (time series, causal)
questions.solution: Quantitative: needs historical data. Qualitative: when data is scarce.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple activity chart:
options: Simultaneous activities of man + machine. Identify idle time. Gang chart
questions.solution: Multiple activity: man-machine ratio. Gang chart: team activities. Balance workload.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Material handling principle: unit load means:
options: Moving items together in one load
questions.solution: Unit load: combine items into single move. Reduces trips, improves efficiency. Palletization, containerization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 10 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect causes:
options: Demand signal processing, order batching, price fluctuation, rationing
questions.solution: Lee et al.: 4 causes. Mitigation: EDI, VMI, ECR, continuous replenishment.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Economic Order Quantity with quantity discount:
options: Compare total cost at each price break
questions.solution: Quantity discount: total cost curve shifts at each break. Compare TC at EOQ and each break point. Choose minimum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Acceptance sampling: AQL (Acceptable Quality Level):
options: Maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable as a process average
questions.solution: AQL: quality level consumer considers acceptable. Producer's risk (alpha) at AQL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root cause analysis (RCA) — 5 Whys:
options: Iterative questioning. Causal chain: physical root → human root → latent root. Corrective action for each
questions.solution: 5 Whys: Toyota Production System. Latent root: organizational factors. Fix root → prevent recurrence.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Constraints Management: non-bottleneck resource:
questions.solution: Non-bottleneck: capacity > demand. Should not be run at full speed. Running full increases inventory.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Warehouse layout: U-flow design provides:
options: Single dock for receiving and shipping
questions.solution: U-flow: receiving and shipping on same side. Efficient use of dock equipment. Good for cross-docking.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: MAPE:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal indices — ratio-to-moving-average:
options: Divide by centered MA. Average seasonal ratios. Adjust to sum to 4 (quarterly)
questions.solution: Seasonal: 12 months or 4 quarters. Centered MA of order 12 or 4. Normalize indices.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a linear programming problem, the set of all points satisfying the constraints is the:
questions.solution: The feasible region is the (convex) set of all solutions meeting every constraint.
